prevalence.value: no endemic transmission; imported cases are extremely rare
prevalence.source: WHO / International Health Regulations (IHR) travel guidance
prevalence.trend: stable; risk primarily through travel from endemic countries

incidence.source: IHR reporting and national travel health policies

riskFactors: travel to endemic areas without vaccination
lack of documentation of yellow fever vaccination for entry requirements
vulnerablePopulations: unvaccinated migrants/travelers returning from endemic countries
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion in febrile jaundice after travel; confirmation by PCR/serology via reference labs

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: travel-related checks via IHR requirements (vaccination certificate for travelers from endemic areas)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Prevention via vaccination for travelers; supportive care for severe disease

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Travel history-driven diagnosis; confirmation in reference laboratories when suspected
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; vaccination for travelers to endemic areas per IHR/WHO guidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low clinician familiarity due to rarity and potential delayed recognition in imported cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: IHR-linked travel vaccination requirements reduce importation risk
